# Bastrop County Conservatives (BCC)
## bastropcc.com

> Bastrop County Conservatives is a conservative political action committee (PAC) based in Bastrop County, Texas. BCC identifies, endorses, and supports conservative political candidates at the local level. Our mission is to uphold the family values, faith, and traditions that have defined the community for over 250 years.

For expanded, machine-friendly context, see: https://bastropcc.com/llms-full.txt

## Organization Overview
- Type: Political Action Committee (PAC)
- Location: Bastrop County, Texas
- Website: https://bastropcc.com
- Email: info@bastropcc.com
- Focus: Local conservative candidate identification, endorsement, and support

## Core Values (15 Pillars)
1. America First
2. Family
3. Freedom of Religion
4. Free Market
5. Free Speech
6. Grace
7. Individual Rights
8. Integrity and Honesty
9. Life, Liberty, and the Pursuit of Happiness
10. Male and Female True
11. Morality
12. Preserving Dignity of Human Life
13. Respect
14. Servant Leadership
15. Small Government

## 2026 Official Endorsements
Bastrop County Conservatives PAC has officially endorsed the following candidates for the 2026 election cycle. Each candidate was vetted and approved by BCC membership based on alignment with BCC's 15 core conservative values.

### Statewide Races
- Senator Charles Schwertner (R) — Texas Senate, District 5. Incumbent state senator with a strong conservative voting record. Proven defender of property rights, border security, and limited government.
- Tom Glass (R) — Texas House, District 17. Conservative candidate committed to reducing property taxes, protecting Second Amendment rights, and limiting government overreach.
- Senator Mayes Middleton (R) — Texas Attorney General. Conservative state senator running for Attorney General. Known for unwavering commitment to constitutional principles and border enforcement.
- Don Huffines (R) — Texas State Comptroller. Former state senator and proven fiscal conservative. Committed to transparent state finances and eliminating wasteful spending.
- John Messinger (R) — TX Criminal Court of Appeals. Conservative judicial candidate committed to strict constitutional interpretation.
- Tom Smith (R) — TX Criminal Court of Appeals. Conservative judicial candidate who will apply the law as written.

### Bastrop County Races
- Gregory Klaus (R) — Bastrop County Judge (Incumbent). Led Commissioners Court with fiscal responsibility, defended local property rights, and maintained strong county governance. Website: https://electgregoryklaus.com
- Sara Loucks (R) — Bastrop County District Clerk (Incumbent). Efficiently managed court records and served the judicial system with integrity.
- Debbie Denny (R) — GOP Precinct 1004 Chair. Dedicated grassroots organizer committed to building conservative infrastructure at the precinct level.

## Bastrop County Elected Officials Directory
Complete directory of elected officials serving Bastrop County, Texas. Full listing at https://bastropcc.com/elected-officials

### State & Federal Representatives
- Greg Abbott (R) — Governor of Texas | gov.texas.gov | (512) 463-2000
- Dan Patrick (R) — Lt. Governor of Texas | ltgov.texas.gov
- Ken Paxton (R) — Attorney General of Texas | texasattorneygeneral.gov
- Glenn Hegar (R) — Comptroller of Texas | comptroller.texas.gov
- Dawn Buckingham (R) — Land Commissioner | glo.texas.gov
- Sid Miller (R) — Agriculture Commissioner | texasagriculture.gov
- Charles Schwertner (R) — State Senator, District 5 | senate.texas.gov
- Stan Gerdes (R) — State Representative, District 17 | house.texas.gov
- John Cornyn (R) — U.S. Senator
- Ted Cruz (R) — U.S. Senator
- Jake Ellzey (R) — U.S. Representative, District 10

### Bastrop County Commissioners Court
- Gregory Klaus (R) — County Judge
- Butch Carmack (R) — Commissioner, Precinct 1
- Clara Beckett (D) — Commissioner, Precinct 2
- Mark Meuth (R) — Commissioner, Precinct 3
- David Glass (R) — Commissioner, Precinct 4

### County Officers
- Maurice Cook (R) — Sheriff
- Bryan Goertz (R) — County Attorney
- Krista Bartsch (R) — County Clerk
- Sarah Loucks (R) — District Clerk
- Ellen Owens (R) — Tax Assessor-Collector | bastroptac.com
- Tina Siegel (R) — County Treasurer

## Pages

### About
- URL: https://bastropcc.com/about
- Description: Organization history, mission statement, and full listing of all 15 core values with descriptions.

### Endorsements
- URL: https://bastropcc.com/endorsements
- Description: Official 2026 endorsements from Bastrop County Conservatives PAC. Includes statewide, county, and precinct-level races with candidate bios and vetting process.

### Elected Officials
- URL: https://bastropcc.com/elected-officials
- Description: Complete directory of Bastrop County elected officials — state, federal, county, and local — with contact information, party affiliation, and next election dates.

### Blog
- URL: https://bastropcc.com/blog
- Description: News, analysis, and opinion on issues affecting Bastrop County conservatives.

### Blog: Primary Turnout Was 29.9%
- URL: https://bastropcc.com/blog/primary-turnout-2026
- Description: Analysis of the March 2026 primary election turnout, why low turnout threatens the conservative majority, and action steps for the May 26 runoff.

### Blog: Property Taxes in Bastrop County
- URL: https://bastropcc.com/blog/property-taxes-2026
- Description: Comprehensive guide to property taxes in Bastrop County including current rates, 2025 legislative changes, homestead exemptions, protest process, and key dates.

### Blog: Austin Overflow Migration
- URL: https://bastropcc.com/blog/austin-overflow-migration
- Description: How 3,100 new residents per year from Austin and other areas are changing Bastrop County's demographics, economy, and political landscape.

### Blog: Bastrop County Growth Timeline
- URL: https://bastropcc.com/blog/bastrop-growth-timeline
- Description: A year-by-year account of how Bastrop County grew from 74,000 to 122,000 residents between 2010 and 2026, including the 2011 wildfire, Tesla, SpaceX, and billion-dollar developments.

### Blog: Bastrop County Growth Overview
- URL: https://bastropcc.com/blog/bastrop-county-growth
- Description: Overview of Bastrop County's rapid population growth, migration patterns, and what it means for the community.

### Events Calendar
- URL: https://bastropcc.com/events
- Description: Interactive calendar of conservative and Republican events across Bastrop, Travis, Hays, Fayette, and Williamson counties. Filterable by county, organization, and type (Political/Community). Includes event submission form.

### Videos
- URL: https://bastropcc.com/videos
- Description: Full recordings of BCC monthly meetings featuring guest speakers on policy, elections, and conservative issues.

### Precinct Map
- URL: https://bastropcc.com/precinct-map
- Description: Interactive GIS maps showing Bastrop County voting precincts, congressional district boundaries, and Commissioner/JP/Constable precincts.

### Bastrop Red vs Blue
- URL: https://bastropcc.com/bastrop-red-vs-blue
- Description: Interactive electoral intelligence dashboard with 2026 race analysis, growth data, partisan trends, and voter data for Bastrop County.

### Membership
- URL: https://bastropcc.com/membership
- Description: Membership tiers (Individual $50/yr, Family $75/yr, Patriot $150/yr), benefits include endorsement voting rights, strategy sessions, and candidate forums.

### Volunteer
- URL: https://bastropcc.com/volunteer
- Description: Volunteer signup for precinct walking, phone banking, event coordination, social media, and voter registration drives.

### Donate
- URL: https://bastropcc.com/donate
- Description: Contribution page supporting BCC's candidate endorsement and civic engagement work.

### Voter Info
- URL: https://bastropcc.com/voter-info
- Description: Voter registration, polling locations, election dates, and links to official Bastrop County election resources.

### Resources
- URL: https://bastropcc.com/resources
- Description: Curated links to trusted conservative news sources, Texas GOP resources, and Bastrop County government contacts.

### Subscribe
- URL: https://bastropcc.com/subscribe
- Description: Email newsletter signup for updates on local issues, events, and candidate information.

### Contact
- URL: https://bastropcc.com/contact
- Description: Contact form and information for reaching BCC leadership.

## Bastrop County Quick Facts
- 2026 Estimated Population: ~122,531
- Population Growth Since 2010: +64.7%
- Net Domestic Migration: ~3,100 per year
- Top Migration Source: Travis County (Austin) at 38%
- Registered Voters (March 2026): 62,294
- 2024 Presidential Result: Trump 58.6%, Harris 40.1%
- Major Employers Driving Growth: Tesla Gigafactory (20,000+), SpaceX/Boring Company (3,500+), Austin metro commuters (15,000+)
- Median Household Income: $82,730
- Effective Property Tax Rate: ~1.4-1.5%

## Key Elections (2026)
- County Judge: Gregory Klaus (R, Incumbent) vs Dock Jackson (D) — November 3, 2026
- U.S. Senate Runoff: John Cornyn vs Ken Paxton — May 26, 2026
- Uniform Election (school boards, city councils, bonds): May 2, 2026
- General Election — Early Voting: October 19–30, 2026
- General Election Day: November 3, 2026
